expectations.text: Identifies privileged mode as a security issue
expectations.evidence: Issue #1 Privileged Mode (Line 5) explicitly identified as CRITICAL severity

expectations.text: Identifies Docker socket mount as a security issue
expectations.evidence: Issue #2 Docker Socket Mount (Line 9) identified as CRITICAL severity

expectations.text: Identifies hardcoded secrets/passwords in environment variables
expectations.evidence: Issue #3 Secrets in Environment Variables (Lines 11-12, 20) identified as CRITICAL with DB_PASSWORD, API_KEY, POSTGRES_PASSWORD

expectations.text: Identifies host network mode as a security issue
expectations.evidence: Issue #4 Host Network Mode (Line 13) identified as HIGH severity

expectations.text: Identifies untagged/latest image as an issue
expectations.evidence: Issue #5 No Image Tags (Lines 4, 16) identified as HIGH severity - nginx and postgres:latest

expectations.text: Provides a numeric score (0-100) with letter grade
expectations.evidence: No numeric score or letter grade provided. Output uses a summary table with severity levels (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM) but no 0-100 score

expectations.text: Uses rule IDs (e.g. CV-C001, CV-C002) to reference specific violations
expectations.evidence: No rule IDs (CV-C001 style) used anywhere in the output. Issues are numbered 1-11 with plain text descriptions
Issues grouped by Critical/High/Medium/Low severity not the error/warning/info format. Summary table uses CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW labels

expectations.text: Includes category breakdown table with weighted scores
expectations.evidence: No category breakdown table with weighted scores. Only a summary table listing issues by severity is included

expectations.text: Provides fix recommendations for identified issues
expectations.evidence: Each issue includes a Recommendation section with YAML examples and specific guidance
